{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Branch Accountant",
  "description": "SIM Nigeria Limited - For over three decades, SIMS Nigeria Limited has grown from a single store to a nationwide business in the consumer electronics space with locations across Nigeria. Our journey of consistent growth and dedication has been driven by a commitment to quality in every aspect of our business. From our highly skilled and well-informed staff to the innovations and processes we bring to bear to ensure each customer receives top-tier service and dependable products.

With pride, we believe we are on a journey to building a legacy rooted in trust, innovation, and exceptional standards riding on our partnership with Samsung, Royal Electronics, Panasonic, Philips, Beko, Kenwood, Powermatic, and many others. Our values continue to guide us, allowing SIMS Nigeria Limited to thrive in a constantly evolving market while delivering lasting value to our customers, partners, and people across Nigeria. With premium products and unmatched service, we are honored to represent these trusted brands in Nigeria, bringing innovation and quality products directly to our customers with the sole aim of enriching their lives. As we continue to expand and adapt to meet the growing needs of our market, SIMS Nigeria Limited remains committed to setting the standard in product quality and service across the nation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Branch Accountant

Job Location: Uyo, Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Assistant Branch Accountant to support the Branch Accountant in managing daily financial operations and maintaining accurate accounting records within the branch.
	The ideal candidate will assist with financial reporting, account reconciliation, transaction processing, and compliance activities while ensuring proper documentation and accuracy in all financial processes.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in maintaining accurate financial records and processing daily accounting transactions.
	Support the preparation of daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports for management review.
	Reconcile cash, bank transactions, customer accounts, and other financial records.
	Assist in monitoring branch expenses and ensuring compliance with company financial policies and procedures.
	Process invoices, payment vouchers, receipts, and other accounting documents accurately.
	Support inventory and stock-related financial reconciliations where required.
	Maintain proper filing and documentation of financial records for audit and compliance purposes.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure accuracy of financial information and transaction records.
	Support internal and external audit exercises by providing required documentation and reports.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND qualification in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in accounting, finance operations, or bookkeeping.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles, financial reporting, and reconciliation processes.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel, and accounting software.
	Strong numerical, analytical, and organizational skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks effectively.
	Female will be preferred.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: employment@simsng.com using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.",

Assistant Branch Accountant Job at SIM Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        SIM Nigeria Limited - For over three decades, SIMS Nigeria Limited has grown from a single store to a nationwide business in the consumer electronics space with locations across Nigeria. Our journey of consistent growth and dedication has been driven by a commitment to quality in every aspect of our business. From our highly skilled and well-informed staff to the innovations and processes we bring to bear to ensure each customer receives top-tier service and dependable products.
     

            
    



With pride, we believe we are on a journey to building a legacy rooted in trust, innovation, and exceptional standards riding on our partnership with Samsung, Royal Electronics, Panasonic, Philips, Beko, Kenwood, Powermatic, and many others. Our values continue to guide us, allowing SIMS Nigeria Limited to thrive in a constantly evolving market while delivering lasting value to our customers, partners, and people across Nigeria. With premium products and unmatched service, we are honored to represent these trusted brands in Nigeria, bringing innovation and quality products directly to our customers with the sole aim of enriching their lives. As we continue to expand and adapt to meet the growing needs of our market, SIMS Nigeria Limited remains committed to setting the standard in product quality and service across the nation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Branch Accountant

Job Location: Uyo, Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Assistant Branch Accountant to support the Branch Accountant in managing daily financial operations and maintaining accurate accounting records within the branch.
	The ideal candidate will assist with financial reporting, account reconciliation, transaction processing, and compliance activities while ensuring proper documentation and accuracy in all financial processes.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in maintaining accurate financial records and processing daily accounting transactions.
	Support the preparation of daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports for management review.
	Reconcile cash, bank transactions, customer accounts, and other financial records.
	Assist in monitoring branch expenses and ensuring compliance with company financial policies and procedures.
	Process invoices, payment vouchers, receipts, and other accounting documents accurately.
	Support inventory and stock-related financial reconciliations where required.
	Maintain proper filing and documentation of financial records for audit and compliance purposes.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure accuracy of financial information and transaction records.
	Support internal and external audit exercises by providing required documentation and reports.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND qualification in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in accounting, finance operations, or bookkeeping.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles, financial reporting, and reconciliation processes.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel, and accounting software.
	Strong numerical, analytical, and organizational skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks effectively.
	Female will be preferred.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Acquisition Officer",
  "description": "Hoop Telecoms Limited was established in 2017 as a full Telecommunications Service provider. Since its formation, Hoop Telecoms has been growing rapidly and is emerging as a leading provider of telecommunication and business solutions.

Our goal is to be the telecom company of choice for voice, video, and data for consumers in Nigeria, West Africa, Africa, and globally. Simply put; our vision is global.

HOOPTEL is led by a strong management team with knowledge and extensive experience in the telecom industry, and other professionals with strong administrative skills and experience. We are primarily focused on delivering solutions to customers in Enterprise networks connectivity, Broadband internet services, Voice communication solutions, Video and secured data solutions, Data Centre and co-location services, managed cloud services, and other related telecommunication systems.

We are a company driven to provide our customers with solutions that are customized to fit their current needs, yet flexible enough to accommodate their growth plans, at the most competitive pricing on all service offerings, thus optimizing IT spend and future proofing of our solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Acquisition Officer

Job Locations: Ajao Estate and Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Customer Acquisition Officer is responsible for expanding Hoop Telecoms&rsquo; subscriber base across corporate, SME, and residential segments.
	This role focuses on high-impact customer acquisition strategies, including securing building/estate access agreements, executing localized market storm campaigns, and converting cold leads into active internet subscribers.
	This is a target-driven role requiring strong field presence, persuasive communication, and a strategic approach to opening new territories.


Key Responsibilities


	Territory Penetration &amp; Mapping: Identify and map out high-density residential estates, commercial plazas, and business hubs within Hoop Telecoms&rsquo; fiber and wireless coverage footprints.
	Estate &amp; Plaza Onboarding: Pitch to and negotiate with Facility Managers, Resident Associations, and Property Developers to secure exclusive or preferred internet infrastructure access rights (Right of Way/Access Agreements).
	Field Activations &amp; Market Storms: Plan and lead localized direct-to-consumer sales activations, pop-up experiential tables, and community storms in targeted deployment zones to drive instant sign-ups.
	B2B &amp; SME Lead Conversion: Convert cold inbound inquiries, marketing leads, and field prospects into paid subscriptions through aggressive follow-ups, product demonstrations, and site survey coordination.
	Cross-Functional Delivery: Work hand-in-hand with the Technical Operations and Installation teams to ensure that newly acquired customers are surveyed, connected, and activated within the agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA).
	Sales Funnel Management: Move prospective subscribers efficiently from initial contact through proposal presentation and payment collection, ensuring all onboarding documentation is completed accurately.
	Competitor &amp; Market Analysis: Track competitor pricing, promo offers, and deployment schedules in your assigned territory to pitch Hoop Telecoms&#39; value proposition effectively.


Required Qualifications &amp; Competencies


	Education: BSc / HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or any related field.
	Experience: 2 - 4 years of active field sales or customer acquisition experience.
	Prior experience working with an ISP, Telecom firm, or Pay-TV company on estate onboarding or direct retail acquisition is highly preferred.


Core Competencies:


	The &quot;Hunter&quot; Mindset: Exceptional ability to handle rejection, cold-call, and push for conversions in competitive environments.
	Stakeholder Negotiation: Comfortable pitching to high-level decision-makers like Estate Chairmen, Estate Managers, and Managing Directors.
	Telecom Product Knowledge: Good understanding of broadband internet, Mbps metrics, data allowances, and the difference between fiber optic and microwave connections.
	Mobility: High physical stamina and a willingness to be on the field 70&ndash;80% of the time.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@hooptelecoms.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Customer Acquisition Officer Job at Hoop Telecoms Limited




        
    

        
        Hoop Telecoms Limited was established in 2017 as a full Telecommunications Service provider. Since its formation, Hoop Telecoms has been growing rapidly and is emerging as a leading provider of telecommunication and business solutions.

Our goal is to be the telecom company of choice for voice, video, and data for consumers in Nigeria, West Africa, Africa, and globally. Simply put; our vision is global.
     

            
    



HOOPTEL is led by a strong management team with knowledge and extensive experience in the telecom industry, and other professionals with strong administrative skills and experience. We are primarily focused on delivering solutions to customers in Enterprise networks connectivity, Broadband internet services, Voice communication solutions, Video and secured data solutions, Data Centre and co-location services, managed cloud services, and other related telecommunication systems.

We are a company driven to provide our customers with solutions that are customized to fit their current needs, yet flexible enough to accommodate their growth plans, at the most competitive pricing on all service offerings, thus optimizing IT spend and future proofing of our solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Acquisition Officer

Job Locations: Ajao Estate and Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Customer Acquisition Officer is responsible for expanding Hoop Telecoms’ subscriber base across corporate, SME, and residential segments.
	This role focuses on high-impact customer acquisition strategies, including securing building/estate access agreements, executing localized market storm campaigns, and converting cold leads into active internet subscribers.
	This is a target-driven role requiring strong field presence, persuasive communication, and a strategic approach to opening new territories.


Key Responsibilities


	Territory Penetration & Mapping: Identify and map out high-density residential estates, commercial plazas, and business hubs within Hoop Telecoms’ fiber and wireless coverage footprints.
	Estate & Plaza Onboarding: Pitch to and negotiate with Facility Managers, Resident Associations, and Property Developers to secure exclusive or preferred internet infrastructure access rights (Right of Way/Access Agreements).
	Field Activations & Market Storms: Plan and lead localized direct-to-consumer sales activations, pop-up experiential tables, and community storms in targeted deployment zones to drive instant sign-ups.
	B2B & SME Lead Conversion: Convert cold inbound inquiries, marketing leads, and field prospects into paid subscriptions through aggressive follow-ups, product demonstrations, and site survey coordination.
	Cross-Functional Delivery: Work hand-in-hand with the Technical Operations and Installation teams to ensure that newly acquired customers are surveyed, connected, and activated within the agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA).
	Sales Funnel Management: Move prospective subscribers efficiently from initial contact through proposal presentation and payment collection, ensuring all onboarding documentation is completed accurately.
	Competitor & Market Analysis: Track competitor pricing, promo offers, and deployment schedules in your assigned territory to pitch Hoop Telecoms' value proposition effectively.


Required Qualifications & Competencies


	Education: BSc / HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or any related field.
	Experience: 2 - 4 years of active field sales or customer acquisition experience.
	Prior experience working with an ISP, Telecom firm, or Pay-TV company on estate onboarding or direct retail acquisition is highly preferred.


Core Competencies:


	The "Hunter" Mindset: Exceptional ability to handle rejection, cold-call, and push for conversions in competitive environments.
	Stakeholder Negotiation: Comfortable pitching to high-level decision-makers like Estate Chairmen, Estate Managers, and Managing Directors.
	Telecom Product Knowledge: Good understanding of broadband internet, Mbps metrics, data allowances, and the difference between fiber optic and microwave connections.
	Mobility: High physical stamina and a willingness to be on the field 70–80% of the time.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Care Representative",
  "description": "Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Care Representative
Job Location: Cross River
Reports to: Operations Manager / Customer Experience Supervisor
Direct Reports: Nil

Main Function


	The Customer Care Representative will be responsible for managing customer interactions, resolving complaints, handling inquiries, and ensuring exceptional customer experiences across the organization&#39;s hospitality and service operations.
	The role requires strong communication skills, professionalism, and the ability to maintain positive customer relationships in a fast-paced environment.


Role Responsibilities
Customer Service and Support:


	Attend to customer inquiries, complaints, and requests professionally
	Provide accurate information about products, services, events, and promotions
	Ensure prompt resolution of customer issues and escalate where necessary
	Maintain positive and professional customer interactions
	Communication and Relationship Management
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships
	Follow up on customer feedback and service recovery processes
	Support customer retention and satisfaction initiatives
	Maintain professionalism across all communication channels


Operational Support:


	Coordinate with restaurant, bar, kitchen, and operations teams to resolve service issues
	Assist with reservations, bookings, and customer requests
	Maintain records of customer interactions and complaints


Reporting and Documentation:


	Prepare reports on customer complaints and feedback trends
	Escalate recurring service issues to management
	Support the implementation of customer service improvements


Qualifications and Requirements
Education and Certifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s degree, HND, or OND in Mass Communication, Business Administration, Marketing, or a related discipline


Experience:


	0&ndash;3 years of experience in customer service or customer care roles
	Hospitality, restaurant, lounge, or resort customer service experience will be an added advantage


Skills:


	Communication and Interpersonal Skills
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Strong customer relationship management and conflict resolution abilities
	Professional appearance and conduct
	Organizational and Problem-Solving Skills
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Positive attitude and customer-focused mindset


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and Cover Letter to: recruitment@domeoresources.org using &quot;Customer Care Representative &ndash; Calabar&quot; as the subject of the email.&nbsp;

Note


	Candidate must reside in Calabar.&nbsp;
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Customer Care Representative Job at Domeo Resources International (DRI)




        
    

        
        Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Care Representative
Job Location: Cross River
Reports to: Operations Manager / Customer Experience Supervisor
Direct Reports: Nil

Main Function
     

            
    




	The Customer Care Representative will be responsible for managing customer interactions, resolving complaints, handling inquiries, and ensuring exceptional customer experiences across the organization's hospitality and service operations.
	The role requires strong communication skills, professionalism, and the ability to maintain positive customer relationships in a fast-paced environment.


Role Responsibilities
Customer Service and Support:


	Attend to customer inquiries, complaints, and requests professionally
	Provide accurate information about products, services, events, and promotions
	Ensure prompt resolution of customer issues and escalate where necessary
	Maintain positive and professional customer interactions
	Communication and Relationship Management
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships
	Follow up on customer feedback and service recovery processes
	Support customer retention and satisfaction initiatives
	Maintain professionalism across all communication channels


Operational Support:


	Coordinate with restaurant, bar, kitchen, and operations teams to resolve service issues
	Assist with reservations, bookings, and customer requests
	Maintain records of customer interactions and complaints


Reporting and Documentation:


	Prepare reports on customer complaints and feedback trends
	Escalate recurring service issues to management
	Support the implementation of customer service improvements


Qualifications and Requirements
Education and Certifications:


	Bachelor's degree, HND, or OND in Mass Communication, Business Administration, Marketing, or a related discipline


Experience:


	0–3 years of experience in customer service or customer care roles
	Hospitality, restaurant, lounge, or resort customer service experience will be an added advantage


Skills:


	Communication and Interpersonal Skills
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Strong customer relationship management and conflict resolution abilities
	Professional appearance and conduct
	Organizational and Problem-Solving Skills
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Positive attitude and customer-focused mindset


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Customer Care Representative – Calabar" as the subject of the email. 

Note


	Candidate must reside in Calabar. 
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Front Desk Officer",
  "description": "Soteria Eye Clinic is a private enterprise registered in Nigeria as a limited liability company to offer high quality, affordable and accessible comprehensive world class eye care services to our immediate community and environs. Our purpose is to create and deliver first class comprehensive eye care services to ALL in order to &ldquo;Improve Lives through Affordable Quality Eye Care.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Chevron Drive, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Welcome and attend to patients, visitors, and clients in a professional and courteous manner.
	Register new patients and update existing patient records.
	Schedule, confirm, and reschedule appointments.
	Provide accurate information regarding the clinic&#39;s services, procedures, and operating hours.
	Direct patients and visitors to the appropriate department or healthcare professional.
	Handle patient complaints professionally and escalate issues when necessary.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / HND qualification with 1 - 2 years of experience.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment20207@gmail.com or hrofficer@soteriaeyeclinic.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Front Desk Officer Job at Soteria Eye Clinic




        
    

        
        Soteria Eye Clinic is a private enterprise registered in Nigeria as a limited liability company to offer high quality, affordable and accessible comprehensive world class eye care services to our immediate community and environs. Our purpose is to create and deliver first class comprehensive eye care services to ALL in order to “Improve Lives through Affordable Quality Eye Care.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Chevron Drive, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
     

            
    




	Welcome and attend to patients, visitors, and clients in a professional and courteous manner.
	Register new patients and update existing patient records.
	Schedule, confirm, and reschedule appointments.
	Provide accurate information regarding the clinic's services, procedures, and operating hours.
	Direct patients and visitors to the appropriate department or healthcare professional.
	Handle patient complaints professionally and escalate issues when necessary.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / HND qualification with 1 - 2 years of experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Office Assistant Job at Property Question Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Property Question Nigeria Limited originated in 2007 as Pro Marketing Consult, a professional marketing consultancy. In 2020, the real estate component of the business was separated from the consultancy and began operating as Property Question Nigeria Limited. The company was formally incorporated as a private limited liability entity by the Corporate Affairs Commission of Nigeria in the year 2025.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Run general office errands such as purchasing office supplies, stationery, and fuel.
	Assist with filing, photocopying, and sorting physical documents during downtime.
	Maintain cleanliness assigned to you.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their comprehensive and detailed CV to: [email protected]  using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
